channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump family will get 75% of crypto token revenue
item.description: The Trump family's cryptocurrency platform, World Liberty Financial, indicated in a report released Thursday that DT Marks DEFI LLC, a Delaware-based company connected to the former president, could get 75 percent of WLF's protocol revenues.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: California Ride-hailing Drivers May Get Back Paid After US Supreme Court Decision
item.description: California ride-hailing drivers may get back paid after a recent US Supreme Court decision clears the way for lawsuits against Uber and Lyft
item.dc:creator: Cal Matters, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple Pay made wallets digital, and it’s only the beginning
item.description: Apple turned your iPhone into your wallet one decade ago. Ten years on, and the company continues to extend the digitization of personal payments, with small-scale loans, car rentals, and everything else that used to occupy space in your pocket.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 7 Android 15 features you can bring to any phone today
item.description: No Android 15? No problem. You can still enjoy some of the fancy new features from Google’s latest Android version — no matter who made your current device or how old it might be.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Spain's i-DE and Ayesa Collaborate on Quantum Technology for Optimizing Electricity Grids
item.description: Ayesa and Spain's electricity distributor, i-DE, collaborate on using quantum technology to optimize the country's power grid.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta partners with horror movie maker Blumhouse Productions to test Movie Gen
item.description: Facebook owner Meta said on Thursday it had teamed up with Blumhouse Productions, the Hollywood company behind popular horror films such as The Purge and Get Out, to put its new generative AI video model Movie Gen to the test.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Musk kicks off campaign swing for Trump in Pennsylvania
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk kicked off a campaign swing across Pennsylvania Thursday in support of former President Trump, underlining the importance of the battleground state in the rapidly nearing election.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: How you can help protect your aging parents from scammers
item.description: "They were raised to be a very trusting generation, a very rule following generation, so they make the assumption that everyone has their best interest at heart," Paula Daigle said.
item.dc:creator: Hayley Wielgus, NewsNation

item.title: Secure Azure Kubernetes with Advanced Container Networking Services
item.description: Microsoft’s Azure Kubernetes Service is designed to take care of much of the day-to-day management of Kubernetes, simplifying a lot of the complexity that goes with building and running large-scale, cloud-native applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Feds seeks more guidance on post-quantum cryptography transition
item.description: Federal cyber experts say they need more formal guidance and a range of other tools for a post-quantum cryptography effort that's expected to cost billions. The post Feds seeks more guidance on post-quantum cryptography transition first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Big Tech turns to nuclear energy to power AI boom
item.description: Major technology companies are increasingly turning to nuclear energy as they attempt to keep up with the vast energy demands of artificial intelligence (AI) without derailing their highly publicized environmental goals.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: SpaceX sues California regulators over rejected launches, claiming political bias
item.description: SpaceX filed a lawsuit against a California commission Tuesday, accusing members of political bias after they rejected the aerospace company’s request to allow more rocket launches from an air base in the state.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: AI-powered scam targets 2.5 billion Gmail users in sophisticated phishing attacks
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about a new AI-powered scam that targets Gmail users and is harder to detect unless you're very cautious.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amazon signs deals to build, invest in small modular nuclear reactors
item.description: Amazon announced a series of deals Wednesday aimed at building out small modular nuclear reactors, becoming the latest major technology company to invest in nuclear energy as Big Tech contends with the extensive energy needs of artificial intelligence (AI).
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Lyft launches voter transportation initiative
item.description: Ride-hailing service Lyft announced it will offer discounted rides, bikeshare and scooters on Election Day, an initiative designed to increase transportation access for voters, according to a press release.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Get started with the free-threaded build of Python 3.13
item.description: The single biggest new feature in Python 3.13 is something Python users have anticipated for ages: a version of Python that allows full concurrency between Python threads by removing the Global Interpreter Lock.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Adobe starts roll-out of AI video tools, challenging OpenAI and Meta
item.description: Facing much larger rivals, Adobe has staked its future on building models trained on data that it has rights to use, ensuring the output can be legally used in commercial work.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Intel, AMD unite in new x86 alliance to tackle AI, other challenges
item.description: Semiconductor rivals Intel and AMD announced the formation of an x86-processor advisory group that will try to address ever-increasing AI workloads, custom chiplets, and advances in 3D packaging and system architectures.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: SUSE Edge upgrade targets Kubernetes and Linux at the edge
item.description: Open source and Linux platform vendor SUSE is looking to help organizations solve some of the complexity and challenges of edge computing with the company’s SUSE Edge 3.1 release, announced today.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Volunteers bring solar power to Hurricane Helene's disaster zone
item.description: BAKERSVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene downed power lines and washed out roads all over North Carolina's mountains, the constant din of a gas-powered generator is getting to be too much for Bobby Renfro.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, KRON4
